title: Bump github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto to v1.3.2
body:
Bumping github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto to v1.3.2 to prevent a low
likelyhood CVE.  Partially addresses:

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1924542

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I think we should wait until APIMachinery gets updated, since that is what is pulling this in.  We have not confirmed that this is a Podman vulnerability, or if the vulnerable code paths are used by Podman.

The two spots that I found that were pulling it in were vendored code, I'm not sure if we are or are not using it under there:  
```
vendor/github.com/Microsoft/hcsshim/Protobuild.toml:  "google/protobuf/descriptor.proto" = "github.com/gogo/protobuf/protoc-gen-gogo/descriptor"
vendor/github.com/containerd/cgroups/stats/v1/metrics.pb.go:	proto "github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto"
vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/types/swarm/runtime/plugin.pb.go:import proto "github.com/gogo/protobuf/proto"
```
We also pull it in a number of files in `tests/tools`, but I'm not worried about those.
Ping. Is the still desired? Consider a LGTM from me in case.
I was hoping we would get this as a side effect of Kubernetes fixing their code, but so far no movement.
We have decided that since we don't use the vulnerable code, to not take this PR.
